reasoning:
The stock price of Southern Company (SO) on 2026-02-01 serves as our baseline ($89.15). Over normal short-horizon periods, stock prices follow a random walk with a slight positive drift, leading to expectations just above 50% for near-horizon positive price returns.

For the first resolution date (2026-02-08, 1 week out), general upward short-term drift holds without any known deterministic negative catalysts, supporting an estimated probability of 0.53.

For the second resolution date (2026-03-03, 1 month out), two significant events occur. First, the Q4 2025 earnings report is expected around mid-February, introducing volatility. Second, SO declared a $0.74 quarterly dividend to shareholders of record on February 17, 2026. Under T+1 settlement rules (and given the Feb 16 market holiday), the stock will trade ex-dividend on Feb 17. This will systematically reduce the stock price by roughly $0.74 (nearly 0.8% of the share price). This deterministic drop outweighs the expected one-month positive drift (typically ~0.5%), shifting the mean expectation slightly negative relative to the baseline. Accordingly, the probability of exceeding the initial price falls slightly below 50% to 0.49.

The probabilities (0.53, 0.49) ensure accurate accounting for the ex-dividend headwind, while satisfying the requirement that uncertainty increases (distance to 0.5 decreases) with longer forecast horizons.
